segments.title: Andromeda's cruiser spirit
segments.summary: The introduction frames the starship Andromeda as a cruiser whose confidence meets an even larger, stronger opponent.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke treats Andromeda's identity as a cruiser as the key to the scene's humour and character. The ship's alarm at encountering something larger captures a familiar cruiser sensibility: capable and self-assured, but not the largest combatant in every fight. It is a brief fictional watch point that uses naval language to set the series' tone.

evidence.note: Dr. Clarke identifies Andromeda as a cruiser and explains why its reaction to a larger opponent fits that role.
